skill.name: cobrapy
skill.description: Guides COBRApy workflows for constraint-based metabolic modeling, including FBA, FVA, knockouts, sampling, SBML I/O, and model validation.
skill.summary: Use COBRApy to analyze genome-scale metabolic models and design reproducible metabolic simulations.
skill.author: K-Dense-AI

security_audit.summary: AI review found the critical heuristic to be a false positive caused by Markdown Python examples with imports, model I/O, plotting, and CSV export. The hardcoded URLs point to COBRApy documentation and an optional K-Dense Web suggestion; no credential access, network execution, or prompt injection evidence was found. Risk is low because users may copy examples that read or write local scientific files.

low_findings.title: Static Code-Execution Flags Are Documentation False Positives
low_findings.description: The analyzer flagged Markdown Python imports and fenced code examples as dynamic imports and shell execution. These locations document COBRApy API usage and do not define executable skill code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines are inside Markdown code fences and show normal Python COBRApy examples. No executable wrapper, shell invocation, or user-input command construction is present.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded External References Without Network Execution
low_findings.description: The network findings are plain documentation links to COBRApy documentation and a K-Dense Web suggestion. They do not make requests, transmit data, or access credentials.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The evidence is limited to visible URLs in Markdown prose. I found no fetch, request, upload, credential access, or automatic network behavior.

low_findings.title: Copied Workflow Examples Can Write Local Output Files
low_findings.description: Several workflow examples export CSV files and plots when users choose to run them. This is expected for scientific analysis, but users should review output paths before execution.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The examples clearly call CSV and image export methods, but only as user-run documentation snippets. This is a minor operational risk rather than malicious behavior.

content.value_statement: Genome-scale metabolic modeling requires careful setup, solver choices, and result validation. This skill guides COBRApy analyses with practical workflows and API references.
actual_capabilities: Explains how to load and save COBRApy models in SBML, JSON, YAML, and MATLAB formats.
Guides flux balance analysis, parsimonious FBA, geometric FBA, and objective changes.
Shows gene and reaction deletion workflows, including single and double knockout studies.
Documents flux variability analysis, flux sampling, production envelopes, and gapfilling patterns.
Provides model validation workflows for feasibility, mass balance, dead ends, duplicates, and loops.
Includes reference patterns for media design, boundary reactions, and model component inspection.
limitations: It provides guidance and examples, not a bundled COBRApy runtime or solver.
Users must provide valid metabolic models and install required Python packages.
Large models, MILP tasks, and sampling workflows can be computationally expensive.
Generated analysis plans still require biological review before lab or production decisions.

use_cases.title: Validate a Genome-Scale Model
use_cases.description: Check feasibility, mass balance, duplicate reactions, dead-end metabolites, and loop behavior before analysis.

use_cases.title: Screen Knockout Strategies
use_cases.description: Design single or double deletion studies and classify genes by predicted growth impact.

use_cases.title: Build Reproducible Teaching Workflows
use_cases.description: Create clear COBRApy exercises for FBA, FVA, media design, and flux sampling.

prompt_templates.title: Load and Inspect a Model
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me load a COBRApy model, inspect reactions, metabolites, genes, objective, medium, and solver settings.

prompt_templates.title: Run FBA and FVA
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a COBRApy workflow for FBA, pFBA, FVA at 90 percent optimum, and key flux interpretation.

prompt_templates.title: Design a Knockout Study
prompt_templates.prompt: Plan a single and double gene knockout analysis that identifies essential genes and synthetic lethal pairs.

prompt_templates.title: Validate and Debug a Model
prompt_templates.prompt: Build a validation workflow for infeasibility, blocked reactions, mass imbalance, dead ends, duplicate reactions, and loop checks.

output_examples.input: Analyze growth after removing candidate genes from an E. coli model.
output_examples.output: A step-by-step knockout workflow with baseline growth, result categories, synthetic lethal screening, and export guidance.

output_examples.input: Compare aerobic and anaerobic minimal media requirements.
output_examples.output: A media design plan that calculates baseline growth, minimal components, shared nutrients, and condition-specific requirements.

output_examples.input: Debug why a metabolic model is infeasible.
output_examples.output: A validation checklist covering medium constraints, open exchanges, blocked reactions, mass balance, dead ends, duplicates, and loops.

best_practices: Use model context managers for temporary objective, medium, bound, and knockout changes.
Check solution status and model feasibility before interpreting flux values.
Save models in SBML when sharing results across tools or teams.
anti_patterns: Do not interpret infeasible or unbounded solutions as biological evidence.
Do not run expensive sampling or double deletion workflows without limiting scope first.
Do not change medium dictionaries in place without reassigning them to the model.

faq.question: Does this skill install COBRApy?
faq.answer: No. It explains COBRApy usage. You must install COBRApy and a compatible solver in your Python environment.

faq.question: Can it work with SBML files?
faq.answer: Yes. The references show how to read and write SBML models with COBRApy I/O functions.

faq.question: Can it help with gene knockout analysis?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers single and double gene deletion workflows and result interpretation.

faq.question: Does it support flux sampling?
faq.answer: Yes. It documents OptGP and ACHR sampling patterns, validation checks, and analysis examples.

faq.question: Is this safe for proprietary biological models?
faq.answer: The skill itself is documentation. Review any generated code before running it on confidential files.

faq.question: Can it replace biological expert review?
faq.answer: No. It supports computational workflow design, but experimental decisions need domain expert review.
